sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to hear (Syr., Ch. id., Arab. سَمِعَ, Eth. ሰምዐ፡), Gen 18:10 Isa 6:9 with an acc. of thing, Gen 3:10 24:52 Exo 2:15 and of pers. speaking, Gen 37:17 1Sa 17:28 followed by כִּי and a whole sentence, Gen 42:2 2Sa 11:26. Specially-(a) to listen (anhören, zuhören), to attend to any person or thing, followed by an acc. Gen 23:8, 11 Gen 23:11, 15 Gen 23:15 Ecc 7:5 אֶל 1Ki 12:15 Isa 46:3, 12 Isa 46:12 לְ Job 31:35 followed by בְּ Job 37:2 but שָׁמַע בְּ is commonly to hear any thing, testis auritus fuit (Plaut.), etwas mit anhören, Gen 27:5 Job 15:8 also, to hear with pleasure, 2Sa 19:36 Psa 92:12.-(b) to hear and answer (used of God), followed by an acc. Gen 17:20 Psa 10:17 54:4 followed by אֶל Gen 16:11 30:22 קֹול פּ׳ Deu 33:7 Psa 5:4 18:7 27:7 28:2 64:2 Lam 3:56 בְּקֹול פּ׳ Gen 30:6 Deu 1:45 אֶל קֹול Gen 21:17. Sometimes also with לְ of the object, Gen 17:20.-(c) to obey, to give heed, Exo 24:7 Isa 1:19 followed by אֶל Gen 28:7 39:10 Deu 18:19 Jos 1:17 לְ Num 14:27 בְּקֹול פּ׳ Gen 27:13 Exo 18:19 Deu 26:14 2Sa 12:18 לְקֹול פּ׳ Gen 3:17 Jud. 2:20 Psa 58:6.

(2) to understand things heard, Gen 11:7 42:23 . לֵב שֹׁמֵעַ an understanding heart, 1Ki 3:9. But אִישׁ שֹׁמֵעַ Pro 21:28 is, “a man who (truly) heard,” a faithful witness, as opp. to a false witness.

Niphal-

(1) to be heard, 1Sa 1:13 followed by לְ (by any one), Neh 6:1, . To be heard is also used for to be regarded, to be cared for, Ecc 9:16 to be heard and answered, Dan 10:12, comp. 2Ch 30:27.

(2) to render obedience, to obey, Psa 18:45.

(3) to be understood, Psa 19:4.

Piel, to cause to hear, i.e. to call, i.q. Hiphil No. 3; with an acc. of pers. and לְ of thing to which any one is called. 1Sa 15:4, “and Saul called all the people to war.” 1Sa 23:8.

Hiphil-

(1) to cause to hear, let hear, as one’s own voice, Jud. 18:25 Son 2:14 (to cause to hear acceptably, Isa 58:4); a cry, Jer 48:4 with two acc. of pers. and thing, to cause any one to hear any thing, 2Ki 7:6 Psa 143:8 followed by אֶל of pers. Eze 36:15. Without קֹול absol. to utter a voice, a cry; hence with the addition of בְּקֹולֹו Psa 26:7 Eze 27:30 (compare נָתַן בְּקֹול); specially to sing, both with the voice, Neh 12:42, and to play on instruments, 1Ch 15:28 16:5 (especially with a loud sound, 1Ch 15:19, compare נִצֵּחַ). Arab. مُسْمِعَةُ a female singer, سِمَاعُ music.

(2) to announce, to tell anything, followed by an acc. of the thing, Isa 45:21 acc. of pers. Isa 44:8 48:5 with two acc. of pers. and thing, Isa 48:6.

(3) to call, to summon, i.q. Piel, 1Ki 15:22 Jer 50:29 51:27 .

Derivatives, שָׁמָע-שִׁמְעָת; also, שְׁמוּעָה, מִשְׁמָע, מִשְׁמַעַת, and pr.n. אֶשְׁתְּמֹעַ, יִשְׁמָעֵאל, יֵשְׁמַעְיָה [See also שְׁמוּאֵל.]
